title: Fix Schema DDL Generation Error with PostgreSQL
story: As a data migration specialist, I want DDL schema generation to work correctly when PostgreSQL is selected as the target database, so that I can generate valid CREATE TABLE scripts for my PostgreSQL environment.
description: The DDL schema generation feature was producing errors when PostgreSQL was selected as the target database type. The same operation worked correctly with MySQL. The fix addresses the PostgreSQL-specific DDL generation logic to produce valid output.
acceptance_criteria: DDL schema generation produces valid output when PostgreSQL is selected
DDL generation continues to work correctly with MySQL
No errors are thrown during PostgreSQL DDL generation
ux_changes: DDL generation now produces valid output for PostgreSQL without error messages.
middleware_changes: Fixed PostgreSQL-specific DDL generation logic that was causing errors in the schema output.
implementation_notes: Bug was specific to PostgreSQL DDL generation while MySQL worked correctly, suggesting a database-type-specific code path issue.
comment_summary: Developer fixed the issue on the development environment.
